verdict: extremely high risk of displacement by AI

reasoning_steps.title: Routine Cognitive Tasks
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks 1, 2, 6, and 9 are all routine cognitive tasks that can be easily automated by AI

reasoning_steps.title: Creative Judgment
reasoning_steps.body: While creative judgment is required in some aspects of radiology, the majority of tasks can be performed with minimal human input

reasoning_steps.title: Regulatory Moat
reasoning_steps.body: The regulatory moat for radiologists is relatively weak due to the lack of licensure requirements and the ability of AI to interpret images

adjacent_careers.name: Radiologic Technologist
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Radiologic technologists perform imaging procedures, but their role is more limited and less complex than that of radiologists

adjacent_careers.name: Medical Imaging Analyst
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Medical imaging analysts interpret images for diagnostic purposes, but their role is more focused on quality control and less on patient diagnosis

recent_developments.headline: New AI Algorithm Improves Image Interpretation in Radiology
recent_developments.impact: This development indicates that AI is becoming increasingly capable of interpreting medical images, potentially displacing human radiologists

recent_developments.headline: FDA clears more AI diagnostic tools each quarter
recent_developments.impact: Radiology, pathology, and dermatology are seeing AI second-readers move into routine clinical workflows.

quotes.quote: AI in medicine should be evaluated like a drug — for safety, for efficacy, and for the population it actually helps.
quotes.speaker: Eric Topol
quotes.title: Cardiologist; Director, Scripps Research Translational Institute

quotes.quote: We should stop training radiologists now. It is just completely obvious that within five years, deep learning is going to do better than radiologists.
quotes.speaker: Geoffrey Hinton
quotes.title: Turing Award laureate; ex-Google
quotes.source: Machine Learning and the Market for Intelligence conference, Toronto

quotes.quote: AI will not replace doctors, but doctors who use AI will replace doctors who don't.
quotes.speaker: Curtis Langlotz
quotes.title: Professor of Radiology and Biomedical Informatics, Stanford
